memo_markdown:
## 1. Recommendation

**PROCEED WITH CONDITIONS.** The committee recommends proceeding, subject to conditions. Base-case equity IRR is 17.18 % [E:E-VAL-IRR] against a minimum DSCR of 1.889 × [E:E-DBT-MIN-DSCR]. Levelised cost of energy is 73.12 EUR/MWh [E:E-VAL-LCOE], framing the merchant downside.

## 2. Asset & Resource

Installed capacity 11.12 [E:E-INP-MW] across 4 [E:E-INP-N-UNITS] turbines.

P50 annual energy is 32.6 GWh [E:E-ENE-P50] at a net capacity factor of 33.47 % [E:E-ENE-NET-CF]; the P90 estimate is 28.64 GWh [E:E-ENE-P90].

## 3. Revenue & Market

Year-one revenue is 2.912 M€ [E:E-REV-Y1] with a wind capture rate of 100.00 % [E:E-REV-CAPTURE].

The award value (anzulegender Wert) is 4.526 ct/kWh [E:E-ASM-AW].

## 4. Financial Structure

| Metric | Value | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Average DSCR | 1.891 × | [E:E-DBT-AVG-DSCR] |
| Sculpted debt capacity | 18,932,970.4 EUR | [E:E-DBT-CAPACITY] |
| Senior debt drawn | 13,010,400 EUR | [E:E-DBT-DRAWN] |
| DSRA funded at close | 433,465.1 EUR | [E:E-DBT-DSRA] |
| Gearing achieved | 0.75 | [E:E-DBT-GEARING] |
| LLCR | 1.924 × | [E:E-DBT-LLCR] |
| Minimum DSCR | 1.889 × | [E:E-DBT-MIN-DSCR] |
| PLCR | 2.305 × | [E:E-DBT-PLCR] |
| Net capacity factor | 0.3347 | [E:E-ENE-NET-CF] |
| P50 annual energy | 32.6 GWh | [E:E-ENE-P50] |
| P75 annual energy | 30.52 GWh | [E:E-ENE-P75] |
| P90 annual energy | 28.64 GWh | [E:E-ENE-P90] |
| 1-year P90 energy (incl. inter-annual) | 27.91 GWh | [E:E-ENE-P90-1YR] |
| Combined uncertainty sigma | 0.09487 | [E:E-ENE-SIGMA] |
| Wind capture rate | 1.0 | [E:E-REV-CAPTURE] |
| CFADS, year 1 | 1,830,242.7 EUR | [E:E-REV-CFADS-Y1] |
| EBITDA, year 1 | 2,032,881.7 EUR | [E:E-REV-EBITDA-Y1] |
| Market revenue, year 1 | 2,912,217.3 EUR | [E:E-REV-MARKET-Y1] |
| Opex, year 1 | 879,335.6 EUR | [E:E-REV-OPEX-Y1] |
| EEG premium revenue, year 1 | 0 EUR | [E:E-REV-PREMIUM-Y1] |
| Revenue, year 1 | 2,912,217.3 EUR | [E:E-REV-Y1] |
| Equity invested | 4,770,265.1 EUR | [E:E-VAL-EQUITY] |
| Equity IRR | 0.1718 | [E:E-VAL-IRR] |
| LCOE (nominal) | 73.12 EUR/MWh | [E:E-VAL-LCOE] |
| NPV @ WACC (unlevered, after tax) | 2,860,208.1 EUR | [E:E-VAL-NPV] |
| Minimum DSCR at P90 energy | 1.54 × | [E:E-VAL-P90-DSCR] |
| Equity payback year | 6 | [E:E-VAL-PAYBACK] |

## 5. Risks & Mitigants

- **[concern]** Gearing is at the cap — debt capacity is leverage-limited.
- **[concern]** Resource uncertainty is wide (atlas-based estimate); P90 sits well below P50.
- **[concern]** A merchant tail follows the end of EEG support — a valuation swing factor.

## 6. Sensitivities

Under P90 energy the minimum DSCR moves to 1.54 × [E:E-VAL-P90-DSCR], the binding downside for the debt sizing.

## 7. Conditions Precedent

- Confirm the gearing cap is acceptable to the credit committee.
- Obtain an independent (metered or Path-A) resource assessment before close.
- Reserve for the merchant-tail years or shorten the debt tenor accordingly.

## Appendix: Assumptions

WACC 5.8 % [E:E-ASM-WACC]; equity hurdle 8 % [E:E-ASM-HURDLE]; senior rate 2.1 % [E:E-ASM-RATE].

Rule-based memo: claims and prose are derived deterministically from the evidence store — no LLM was used. Figures trace to the citation seals.

claims.statement: Net capacity factor lands at 33.5% after farm losses, a credible P50 for this site class.
Assumed energy availability of 97% is consistent with modern full-service O&M contracts.
Combined resource uncertainty of sigma 0.095 pulls P90 well below P50; a Path-B (atlas) estimate carries wider error than metered data.
Turbine type GE 2.75 120 (commissioned 2017) is a modern platform with a mature service ecosystem.
Wind capture rate of 100% of baseload reflects the cannibalisation discount wind earns in high-wind hours.
Revenue rests on the EEG market premium, which floors the anzulegender Wert but transfers merchant risk after the support window.
The §51 rule voids the premium only in negative streaks of 6+ hours, a moderate exposure for this vintage.
Support ends at year 20 while the asset runs to year 25; the merchant tail is a real valuation swing factor.
Minimum DSCR of 1.89x sits 0.69x above the 1.20x covenant, giving real headroom.
Under P90 energy the minimum DSCR falls to 1.54x — coverage survives the resource downside.
Gearing reaches 75% — the cap binds, so debt capacity is leverage-limited, not cash-limited.
LLCR of 1.92x indicates loan-life coverage with comfortable margin.

rebuttals.statement: Credit view: the 1.89x coverage headroom absorbs this resource concern within the base-case sculpt; it is a pricing input, not a dealbreaker.
